office_does: The auditor is the county's chief financial officer and administers elections, licensing, recording, and related public services.
race_blurb: Four candidates contrast direct auditor-office finance experience with elected legislative, county, and municipal management backgrounds.

candidates.summary: Deputy auditor and former assistant state auditor emphasizing nonpartisan administration, finance, accuracy, and transparency.
highlights: Only candidate with direct Clark County Auditor management experience [S1][S2]

spending.basis: Emphasizes financial oversight and operational accuracy.

experience.basis: Direct deputy-auditor management and government-finance record.

candidates.summary: Former county council chair and state legislator emphasizing voter-list maintenance, safeguards, and waste and fraud review.
highlights: Proposes voter-list maintenance and searches for waste, fraud, and abuse [S1][S2]
Has county and state legislative experience [S1]

spending.basis: Explicit waste, fraud, abuse, and fiscal-oversight posture.

experience.basis: Extensive elected county and legislative record, though no election-administration tenure.

candidates.summary: Longtime state representative emphasizing safe elections, secure records, fiscal stewardship, privacy, and modern tools.
highlights: Emphasizes fiscal stewardship and accountable modern records systems [S1]

spending.basis: Emphasizes stewardship and accountability rather than expansion.

experience.basis: Long legislative and county-operations record but no direct auditor tenure.

candidates.summary: Longtime Vancouver councilmember and private-sector systems manager emphasizing secure elections, records, staff, and responsible resources.
highlights: Has eleven years of municipal elected service [S1]

spending.basis: Responsible resource management and systems oversight support a restraint direction.

experience.basis: Long municipal elected and executive-management experience.
